title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — June 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. Energy components validated with AAA National Average (EIA API 403). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: June CPI headline +0.1% m/m (consensus +0.2%) on energy deflation. Core +0.2% m/m (consensus +0.2-0.3%). WTI collapsed to ~$68-70 in June (-35% from $107 peak). AAA gas $3.85 (-11% from May avg $4.32). Core PPI printed +0.8% m/m Jun 25 — passthrough risk to core services. Fed dot plot 3.8% = one hike priced for 2026. Warsh hawkish tone limits soft-print reaction. Energy deflation dominates headline; watch core PPI passthrough.
baseCase: Below-consensus headline CPI on energy deflation; core stable at +0.2% but FOMC hawkishness and hot PPI caps market reaction

aggregation.verification: Food + Energy + Core = 100.000% AND Shelter + CoreGoods + CoreServices = 80.557%

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: WTI crashed from ~$107 to ~$68-70 in June (-35%). AAA gas $3.85 vs May avg $4.32 (-11%). Energy CPI deflation ≈ -2.0% m/m composite. Exact BLS weights: Food 13.25% + Energy 6.19% + Core 80.56%. Core stable (+0.2%) with shelter cooling but services sticky. Core PPI +0.8% m/m Jun 25 = passthrough risk. Fed dot plot 3.8% median = one hike in 2026. Warsh hawkish hold limits soft-CPI market reaction.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — below consensus on oil deflation, core PPI passthrough muted
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields stable-to-fade (4.40-4.45%), DXY 101-102 range. Airlines (UAL) and homebuilders (DHI) rally on energy/mortgage relief. Tech (NVDA) limited upside due to FOMC hawkishness. Gold (GLD) under pressure from DXY strength.
scenarios.invalidation: Core PPI +0.8% passthrough fully realized; core services re-accelerate above +0.30%; Iran deal collapses.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.3% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — core PPI passthrough, services sticky, oil partial rebound
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields spike (4.50%+), DXY 102+. Tech (NVDA) and REITs (AMT) selloff. USD long, energy (XLE) short-cover rally. Gold (GLD) squeeze on real-yield uncertainty.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by one-off energy component; core services actually stable; PPI passthrough delayed.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.1% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m — oil collapse fully captured, DXY compression, PPI passthrough absent
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drop (4.25-4.35%), DXY 100-101. Growth (QQQ) rallies, airlines (UAL) +10-15%. Homebuilders (DHI) on mortgage relief. Energy (XLE) -5%. TLT duration play.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome; FOMC hawkish tone prevents rally; PPI passthrough emerges late.

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.2% and services ex-shelter stable; 10Y yield fails 4.50%
setups.invalidation: Sticky core services above +0.30% with core PPI passthrough accelerating.

setups.bias: Cautious — FOMC hawkish + hot PPI limits multiple expansion
setups.trigger: 10Y yield drops below 4.40% after release and DXY softens below 101
setups.invalidation: Hot CPI with 10Y yield above 4.55% and DXY above 102.

setups.bias: Long — jet fuel collapse + summer demand
setups.trigger: WTI $65-72 and CPI headline soft; summer travel bookings strong
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI above $80, or travel demand disappoints.

setups.bias: Long — mortgage rate relief on soft CPI + 10Y fade
setups.trigger: 10Y yield below 4.40% and core CPI inline or cooler
setups.invalidation: 10Y yields spike above 4.55% on hot CPI or PPI passthrough.

setups.bias: Short — oil supply glut, Iran deal progress, demand weak
setups.trigger: WTI below $72 and Hormuz traffic normalizes; API inventory weak demand
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, Israel attacks, or OPEC+ cuts production.

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.note: FOMC hawkish hold on Jun 16-17. Warsh first meeting. 9/18 policymakers expect hike. PCE forecasts revised up to 3.6%.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.40% = growth rally. Above 4.55% = risk-off. Oil pulling down, FOMC + PPI pushing up.

watchItems.label: WTI Oil / Energy Data
watchItems.note: WTI crashed -35% in June. AAA gas $3.85 (Jul 1) vs $4.32 (May). EIA API 403 — AAA used as primary. BACKTEST WARNING: WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI (R²=0.23). Energy weight only 6.193% but gasoline swing is large.

watchItems.label: Core PPI
watchItems.note: 2.7x above consensus (+0.3%). Passthrough risk to core CPI with 1-3 month lag. Watch wholesale services and goods.

watchItems.label: DXY
watchItems.note: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough.

watchItems.label: Data Quality / Backtest
watchItems.note: Backtest (15 months): exact BLS weights Core MAE 0.035%, Headline MAE 0.146%. WTI→Gasoline model failed (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). AAA National Average used as EIA API unavailable.

keyDrivers: Oil crash: WTI -35% in June ($107→~$68-70) = gasoline CPI -6% to -8% m/m. AAA national average $3.85 vs May $4.32 (-11%). Energy deflation is the dominant headline driver. EIA API 403 — AAA validated.
Core PPI hot: +0.8% m/m Jun 25 (2.7x consensus). Passthrough risk to core CPI with 1-3 month lag. Wholesale services and goods feeding through.
FOMC hawkish hold: Warsh's first meeting. Dot plot 3.8% median = one hike in 2026. PCE forecasts revised up to 3.6%. Limits soft-CPI market reaction.
DXY 101.40: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough. Core goods may see mild deflation.
Core services sticky: Retail sales resilient, wages +3.6% YoY. But shelter decelerating (Zillow/Apartment List). OER normalizing to ~0.25% m/m.
risks: Core PPI passthrough: Jun +0.8% m/m PPI feeds into core CPI with 1-3 month lag. If passthrough is faster than expected, core undershoot narrative breaks.
Iran deal collapse: Nuclear inspectors denied, Israel/Lebanon tensions, US Congress opposition. WTI $80+ = CPI revision to hotter.
FOMC hawkish limits reaction: Market may 'sell the rally' even on soft CPI. Warsh 'one data point' rhetoric. One hike in 2026 priced.
Demand weakness: Global recession fears rising. If oil and risk assets both fall, soft CPI = bad news for equities.
Shelter lag uncertainty: Zillow/Apartment List slowing but BLS 6-12 month lag. Faster deceleration = core downside surprise.
